test_run_actual.note: Live run via `npx playwright test --reporter=list`. The '47 failing' figure in the task prompt was stale; live run produced 40 failures.

items.error: Strict mode violation — locator matches 2 elements: 'Brands' and 'For Brands'
items.root_cause: Test uses `getByRole('link', { name: 'Brands' })` without `exact: true`. This matches both 'Brands' (/brands) and 'For Brands' (/for-brands). The nav IS correct — the selector is ambiguous.
items.fix: Add `{ exact: true }` to the getByRole call: `getByRole('link', { name: 'Brands', exact: true })`

items.error: Expected href '/for-buyers' but actual href is '/professionals'
items.root_cause: The nav link label 'For Buyers' correctly links to '/professionals' (the professionals page is the for-buyers destination). The routeTable in navigation.spec.ts has the wrong expected href. The live nav shows: `link 'For Buyers' → /url: /professionals` — this is intentional product routing, not a bug.
items.fix: Update REQUIRED_NAV_LINKS in navigation.spec.ts: change `{ label: 'For Buyers', href: '/for-buyers' }` to `{ label: 'For Buyers', href: '/professionals' }` OR update REQUIRED_NAV_LINKS to match whatever the actual nav route spec is. Separately verify with owner whether /for-buyers or /professionals is canonical.
items.fix_owner: TEST team (selector fix) — also flag to PRODUCT to clarify canonical URL
items.note: Route /for-buyers exists and renders safely (passes route crawl). The nav just links 'For Buyers' to /professionals instead. This may be intentional aliasing.

items.error: Expected href '/pricing' but actual href is '/plans'
items.root_cause: The nav 'Pricing' link routes to '/plans', not '/pricing'. Both routes exist (route crawl passes both). The test expects /pricing but the nav implementation uses /plans. CLAUDE.md §C nav spec shows `{ to: '/plans', label: 'Pricing' }` — so /plans IS the canonical destination and the test spec is wrong.
items.fix: Update REQUIRED_NAV_LINKS in navigation.spec.ts: change `{ label: 'Pricing', href: '/pricing' }` to `{ label: 'Pricing', href: '/plans' }`

items.root_cause: The homepage uses a generic div-based layout without a semantic `<main>` element. The page snapshot confirms the top-level structure is `generic [ref=e3]` → `generic [ref=e25]` (content area) — no `<main>` tag. This IS a real accessibility gap but was classified by the test author as a landmark check. However, the page loads and works — it is a missing semantic element, not a broken flow.
items.fix: Add `<main id="main-content">` wrapper to PublicHome component. This is a 5-minute fix that improves accessibility and makes the test pass.
items.note: Reclassified as Type B because the missing <main> is a genuine WCAG issue, not a test selector problem. See Type B section.

items.root_cause: Missing meta description tag on /intelligence page. SITE-WO-04 is open (SEO coverage 40.1%). This is a real product gap — the test is correctly written and checking a valid requirement.
items.fix: Add react-helmet-async meta description to Intelligence.tsx page component.
items.note: Reclassified as Type B — see Type B section.

items.root_cause: Missing meta description on /brands page. Part of broader SITE-WO-04 gap.
items.fix: Add react-helmet-async meta description to PublicBrands.tsx

items.root_cause: Missing meta description on /education page.
items.fix: Add react-helmet-async meta description to Education.tsx

items.fix: Exclude auth pages from SEO meta desc test scope OR add generic meta tag
items.fix_owner: TEST team — auth pages should be excluded

items.root_cause: No OG meta tags implemented on homepage. SITE-WO-04 is open.

items.error: og:description content is null on homepage
items.root_cause: No OG meta tags on homepage.

items.error: og:type content is null on homepage
items.root_cause: No OG meta tags on homepage.

items.test_name: SEO — Open Graph Tags — /intelligence has og:title, og:description, og:type
items.root_cause: No OG meta tags on /intelligence page. Three tests (og:title, og:description, og:type).

items.test_name: SEO — Open Graph Tags — /brands has og:title, og:description, og:type
items.root_cause: No OG meta tags on /brands page. Three tests.

items.root_cause: No OG meta tags on /education page. Three tests.

items.error: Console errors detected on homepage load
items.root_cause: The route crawl test collects console errors and page errors, then asserts `consoleErrors.toEqual([])`. The homepage generates console errors — confirmed via the error-context.md page snapshot showing a fully rendered homepage, meaning the page loads but emits JS console errors. This constitutes real noise a developer would see on the console. Exact error text not captured in list-mode output but the test-results folder exists for /.
items.severity: P2 — Console errors on homepage landing page are a product bug. They may be Supabase auth 401s (which routes.spec.ts ignores by pattern) or other non-ignored errors.
items.fix: Investigate and resolve console errors on the homepage. Run `npx playwright show-trace` on the trace.zip to see exact error text.

items.error: Application Error: 'isSupabaseConfigured is not a function'
items.root_cause: The /events page throws a runtime crash: `isSupabaseConfigured is not a function`. The page shows an error boundary with 'The application encountered an error and could not continue.' This is a real product bug — a user visiting /events would see a full crash screen, not a demo page. The function `isSupabaseConfigured` is being called as a function but is likely exported as a boolean value (or vice versa) in the events component.
items.severity: P0 — Full page crash visible to end users
items.fix: Find `isSupabaseConfigured` import in the Events component and fix the usage — either change the call `isSupabaseConfigured()` to `isSupabaseConfigured` (if it's a boolean) or fix the export to be a function.

items.test_name: DEMO surfaces display visible DEMO or PREVIEW badges
items.error: Expected DEMO/PREVIEW label on /events — body text contains none of: 'demo', 'preview', 'coming soon', 'sample'
items.root_cause: The /events page crashes with a full application error (see route crawl failure above), so no DEMO badge is visible. Additionally, even if the crash were fixed, the events page (DEMO per CLAUDE.md §E) must surface a DEMO label. This is a product bug — per §8 LIVE vs DEMO doctrine: MOCK (unlabeled) is FORBIDDEN. Even if crash is fixed, the DEMO label requirement remains.
items.severity: P0 — Unlabeled DEMO page violates §8 Stop Condition. Related to /events crash.
items.fix: Fix /events crash first. Then add DEMO banner to Events.tsx as required by §8.

items.error: No <main> or [role='main'] element found on homepage
items.root_cause: The homepage uses generic div containers without a semantic `<main>` element. The page snapshot confirms the content area is a `generic` (div) element rather than `<main>`. This is a genuine WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.1 and 2.4.1 compliance gap — screen reader users cannot navigate to main content via keyboard shortcut. It also blocks the skip-to-main-content link from functioning.
items.severity: P2 — WCAG accessibility violation. Blocks launch gate §16 item 16 (Playwright smoke tests pass).
items.fix: Wrap the main content section in PublicHome.tsx with `<main id='main-content'>...</main>`.

items.test_name: Meta Descriptions — 16 content pages missing <meta name='description'>
items.error: meta description content is null on: /intelligence, /brands, /education, /jobs, /for-buyers, /for-brands, /professionals, /for-medspas, /for-salons, /pricing, /plans, /how-it-works, /about, /faq, /protocols, /request-access, /stories, /privacy, /terms
items.root_cause: SITE-WO-04 (SEO) is open. SEO coverage is 40.1% — 163 of 272 pages lack meta tags. These are real missing meta descriptions on content pages that users and search engines visit. The tests correctly identify a genuine product gap that is a launch non-negotiable (§16 item 12: 'SEO baseline complete').
items.severity: P3 — Required for launch. Maps to SITE-WO-04.
items.fix: Implement react-helmet-async meta tags on all content pages as part of SITE-WO-04.
items.note: The 2 auth pages (/forgot-password, /reset-password) failing this test are borderline — they are classified as Type A because auth pages have limited SEO value. The 16+ content pages are Type B.

items.test_name: Open Graph Tags — 4 key pages missing og:title, og:description, og:type (12 tests total)
items.root_cause: No Open Graph meta tags implemented on any key page. These are the 4 pages the test author identified as highest priority for social sharing. Real product gap per SITE-WO-04.
items.severity: P3 — Required for launch social sharing. Maps to SITE-WO-04.
items.fix: Add og:title, og:description, og:type to PublicHome, Intelligence, PublicBrands, Education page components.

items.test_name: business login/logout/session restore; admin login can access admin routes; brand login can access brand routes
items.error: Skipped — E2E_BUSINESS_EMAIL, E2E_ADMIN_EMAIL, E2E_BRAND_EMAIL env vars not set
items.root_cause: These tests are conditionally skipped with `test.skip(!BUSINESS_EMAIL || !BUSINESS_PASSWORD, ...)`. Not failures — they are intentional skips. However, the credentials not being configured in CI means these critical auth flows have zero E2E coverage in the current run.
items.severity: P1 — No E2E coverage of the authenticated portal flows. This is a CI/CD configuration gap.
items.fix: Configure E2E_BUSINESS_EMAIL, E2E_ADMIN_EMAIL, E2E_BRAND_EMAIL, and corresponding password env vars in the test environment. Use test-only accounts (not the platform_admin account).
items.note: Classified as Type B because missing CI credentials is a real operational gap, not a test code problem.

overall_assessment: 40 failures across 215 tests (18.6% failure rate). The failures split 5 Type A (test code bugs) / 35 Type B (real product gaps). The most critical finding is the /events page crash ('isSupabaseConfigured is not a function') which shows a full error boundary to users — P0. The bulk of failures (35) are missing SEO meta tags across content pages — all traceable to the open SITE-WO-04 work order. Three navigation tests fail due to stale href expectations in the test spec (Type A). The /forgot-password and /reset-password meta description failures are borderline but classified Type A since auth pages are low-SEO-value. Auth-gated E2E tests (3 skipped) need CI credentials configured.

recommended_actions.action: Fix /events page crash: `isSupabaseConfigured is not a function`
recommended_actions.note: Find the isSupabaseConfigured import in Events.tsx or its dependencies and fix the call — likely called as a function when it exports a boolean value.

recommended_actions.action: Add DEMO badge to /events and /jobs pages
recommended_actions.note: Per CLAUDE.md §8, DEMO surfaces must be labeled. /events and /jobs are DEMO pages per SOCELLE-WEB operational context §E truth table.

recommended_actions.note: Auth pages have no SEO value. Update the publicRoutes filter in seo.spec.ts.
